sections.paragraph:
ἀποπλανάω fut. ἀποπλανήσω Sir 13:6; 1 aor. ἀπεπλάνησα LXX, pass. ἀπεπλανήθην (s. πλανάω; Hippocr. et al.; Ps.-Pla., Ax. 369d; Polyb. 3, 57, 4; Dionys. Hal.; Plut.; Epict. 4, 6, 38; Sb 7464, 6; LXX; En; TestSol 8:9; TestReub 4:1 [some mss. omit]; Just., D. 124, 3) mislead τινά (2 Ch 21:11; Pr 7:21; Sir 13:6; En 98:15) only fig. of false teachers ἀ. τ. ἐκλεκτούς mislead the elect Mk 13:22; cp. Hm 5, 2, 1; Pol 6:3. Pass. (Sir 4:19; 13:8; 2 Macc 2:2) ἀποπλανᾶσθαι ἀπό τινος wander away fr. someone or someth. ἀ. ἀπὸ τ. πίστεως they have gone astray fr. the faith 1 Ti 6:10 (Dionys. Hal., Comp. Verb. 4 ἀπὸ τ. ἀληθείας). ἀποπλανώμενος ἀπὸ τ. θεοῦ wandered away fr. God Hs 6, 3, 3; ἀ. ἀπὸ τ. διανοίας αὐτῶν wander away fr. their understanding Hm 10, 1, 5. Abs. be led into error (En 8:2) Hs 9, 20, 2. τὰ ἀποπεπλανημένα = τοὺς ἀ. those who have gone astray Pol 6:1.—DELG s.v. πλανάομαι. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hm Hm = Mandates

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

  1. fr. = from

someth. someth. = something

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 118
